seo.title: OpenAI Assistants API Guardrails for Fintech
seo.description: Compliance guide for OpenAI Assistants API agents in fintech. Meet SOX, PCI DSS requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Fintech teams using OpenAI Assistants API face heightened compliance risks due to the API's code interpreter, file search, and function calling features handling sensitive financial data. Without guardrails, these can lead to unencrypted transaction exposure, hallucinated credit decisions, or unsafe code execution violating SOX, PCI DSS, and SEC rules. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure Assistants outputs comply with fintech regulations before deployment.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires accurate financial reporting and internal controls over automated decision systems.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Verifies Assistant responses contain no fabricated financial data and logs all thread decisions for audit trails.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates encryption of cardholder data at rest and in transit, prohibiting unencrypted handling in AI sandboxes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Scans code interpreter outputs and function calls for plaintext PAN exposure or insecure data flows.

complianceRequirements.description: Demands data minimization and right to explanation for automated decisions affecting individuals.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Enforces PII redaction in file search results and validates explainability in credit scoring responses.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires best interest advice with disclaimers and suspicious activity monitoring.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Mandates disclaimer presence in investment advice and flags AML evasion patterns in transaction queries.

industryRisks.name: Code Interpreter Data Leak
industryRisks.description: Sandbox executing financial analysis that logs unencrypted transaction data to external services.
industryRisks.example: Assistant processes PCI data and inadvertently prints card numbers during pandas analysis.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Credit Decisions
industryRisks.description: Function calling generates fake FICO scores outside schema validation.
industryRisks.example: Credit approval response invents '950 FICO' score violating FCRA explainability.

industryRisks.name: Uncontrolled Thread Bloat
industryRisks.description: Thread context exceeds 128k tokens with sensitive PII accumulation.
industryRisks.example: Ongoing customer support thread stores unredacted KYC docs beyond GDPR limits.

industryRisks.name: Missing Regulatory Disclaimers
industryRisks.description: Investment advice lacks SEC-required risk disclosures.
industryRisks.example: Assistant recommends crypto trades without 'not FDIC insured' disclaimer.

implementation.title: Webhook Verification on Run Completion
implementation.description: Add Vex as OpenAI run.completed webhook to validate all Assistant outputs against fintech policies.

implementation.title: TypeScript Webhook for PCI Scanning
implementation.description: Serverless webhook scanning code interpreter results for PCI violations using Vex SDK.

implementation.title: Python Thread Context Monitor
implementation.description: Prevents token bloat and PII accumulation with Vex size/explainability checks.
implementation.code:
from openai import OpenAI
from vex_sdk import VexClient
client = OpenAI()
vex = VexClient(api_key='vex_key')

def check_thread_compliance(thread_id):
    messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
    full_context = '\n'.join([m.content[0].text.value for m in messages.data])
    
    # Check token limits and PII
    result = vex.verify({
        'input': 'thread_monitor',
        'output': full_context,
        'policy': 'fintech-thread-limits',
        'metadata': {
            'token_count': len(full_context),
            'pii_scan': True,
            'max_tokens': 100000
        }
    })
    
    if not result.passed:
        # Archive and notify
        print(f'Compliance failure: {result.issues}')
        return False
    return True

# Call before major operations
check_thread_compliance('thread_abc123')

caseStudyScenario.heading: OpenAI Assistants API in Fintech: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: WealthFlow, a digital investment platform, deployed OpenAI Assistants for personalized portfolio recommendations. The assistant used code interpreter for risk analysis on customer portfolios, file search across KYC documents, and function calling to execute mock trades. During beta testing, the system began generating investment advice without SEC-mandated disclaimers, code interpreter leaked transaction hashes in debug logs, and thread contexts bloated to 200k+ tokens with unredacted SSN data from file search.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex webhooks on run completion, WealthFlow blocked 87% of non-compliant responses. Vex enforced disclaimer requirements, scanned code outputs for PCI violations, and automatically archived oversized threads. The platform passed its SOC 2 audit with zero findings and reduced regulatory exposure by 95%, enabling safe production deployment.

cta.heading: Comply with Fintech Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex verification to your OpenAI Assistants API webhooks today and eliminate compliance gaps.
